message:
fix(termwiz): correct ClearToEndOfLine/Screen on non-BCE terminals

Two long-standing FIXME comments noted that ClearToEndOfLine and
ClearToEndOfScreen did not work correctly on terminals without the
back-color-erase (BCE) capability. The renderer had no cursor-position
tracking so it could not emit the correct number of colored spaces.

Changes:
- Add cursor_position: Option<(usize, usize)> tracking to TerminfoRenderer,
  updated on every move_cursor_absolute, CursorHome, Text output, and
  CursorPosition change; set to None after ambiguous operations
- Add update_cursor_position() resolving Relative/Absolute/EndRelative
- Add update_cursor_position_for_text() using finl_unicode grapheme
  clusters for accurate column counting with unicode-width awareness
- Extract write_spaces() helper
- ClearToEndOfLine non-BCE: emit (cols - cursor_x) spaces then restore
  cursor via \r + cursor_right
- ClearToEndOfScreen non-BCE: fill rest of current line, iterate
  remaining rows with \r\n + full-width spaces, restore cursor
- ClearScreen: explicit move_cursor_absolute(0,0) after space-fill
- Two new tests: clear_to_end_of_line_no_bce, clear_to_end_of_screen_no_bce

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
